pattern.styleContract: Craft-forward and material honest with stain-window proof, close wood-grain work evidence, tool-gold action accents, and a bench ticket as the signature move.

tagline: Wood staining that protects the good grain.

hero.title: CedarLine deck and fence staining hero image
hero.alt: A wood staining professional brushing rich stain onto cedar deck boards beside a freshly stained privacy fence in a bright backyard.

contact.serviceArea: Deck and fence staining, prep, color refresh, and maintenance guidance for cedar, pine, pressure-treated wood, railings, gates, privacy fences, and backyard structures across the central metro and nearby suburbs

copy.eyebrow: Deck and fence staining with clean edges
copy.heroTitle: Bring the grain back before weather writes over it.
copy.heroText: CedarLine washes, preps, masks, and stains decks and fences with tidy brush work, smart dry-time planning, and color notes that make the wood feel intentional again.
copy.introTitle: A wood-care bench, not a paint splash.
copy.introText: We check shade, moisture, loose boards, previous coatings, railing detail, fence exposure, and access before recommending a stain path.
copy.processTitle: From photo check to final board
copy.proofTitle: Craft notes that protect the finish
copy.quoteTitle: Ready to line up a stain window?
copy.quoteText: Send photos of the deck or fence, rough dimensions, stain preference, and when the area gets shade. We will reply with prep notes, timing, and the cleanest quote path.

services.title: Deck Grain Refresh
services.text: Wash, light prep, masked edges, railing attention, semi-transparent stain, and a tidy handoff for decks that need color without hiding the wood.

services.title: Fence Color Reset
services.text: Fence-board cleaning, gate and post detail, controlled spray or brush work, and color guidance for long runs that need a consistent finish.

services.title: Prep And Protection
services.text: Moisture checks, problem-board notes, previous-coating review, plant protection, and dry-time planning before stain day gets on the calendar.

process.title: Send board photos
process.text: Photos of the boards, rails, shaded areas, stairs, gates, and peeling spots help us spot prep needs before quoting.

process.title: Test the path
process.text: We confirm access, moisture, washing needs, color family, previous coating behavior, and a weather window that makes sense.

process.title: Stain and tidy up
process.text: The crew masks edges, works with the grain, protects nearby plants and siding, and leaves simple care notes before packing up.

proof.label: controlled application around rails and edges
prep and color guidance before quote

notes: Works: a bench ticket. gives the page a memorable shape.
Proof: material/process closeup with craft detail. anchors the niche in the first viewport.
Action: Send wood photos is the main conversion path.

brandBrief.accentBehavior: sun stays reserved for Send wood photos, active proof cues, and key booking moments.
brandBrief.avoidLookingLike: Avoid a generic workshop-bench recolor; do not repeat stock hero framing, copied card rhythm, or vague local-service claims.

serviceDetails.turnaround: 48 hr: weather-window planning before stain day.

serviceDetails.prepNote: Photos of the boards, rails, shaded areas, stairs, gates, and peeling spots help us spot prep needs before quoting.
serviceDetails.serviceRhythm: photo first for Deck and fence staining, prep, color refresh, and maintenance guidance for cedar, pine, pressure-treated wood, railings, gates, privacy fences, and backyard structures across the central metro and nearby suburbs.
serviceDetails.objectionAnswer: Scope, access, timing, and fit are confirmed before booking so the next step is clear and low-pressure.
